subject: Changes drl files
commit_message:
Changes drl files

Changes to the DRL file to change the rules to support custom query.
Added new VdnsControlLoop test for custom query, and supporting yaml
request

Issue-ID: POLICY-1858
Change-Id: Ic1b9e771bd36fe0642e9a3439189076a4906a4b8
Signed-off-by: pramod.jamkhedkar <pramod@research.att.com>

Thanks for the comments!

"might want to consider moving this to a separate class so it can be invoked from all drl files"
I am not sure what exactly you mean by this.

Patch Set 1:

> > Patch Set 1:
 > >
 > > (8 comments)
 > Thanks for the comments!
 > 
 > "might want to consider moving this to a separate class so it can
 > be invoked from all drl files"

extract that block of code into a common function that all of the drl files can reference

Patch Set 1:

> Patch Set 1:
> 
> > > Patch Set 1:
>  > >
>  > > (8 comments)
>  > Thanks for the comments!
>  > 
>  > "might want to consider moving this to a separate class so it can
>  > be invoked from all drl files"
>  > I am not sure what exactly you mean by this.
> 
> extract that block of code into a common function that all of the drl files can reference

I see. I could do that and move this block of code to a function in EventManager (seems to me the most logical place, unless you have another suggestion). One comment though -- This code only exists to handle both named Query and Custom Query. From what I understand, named query will soon be removed, and once you remove the named query part of this code, it is essentially a just single function call. Should I still move it to another class?

Patch Set 1:

> > Patch Set 1:
 > >

 > drl files can reference

 > I see. I could do that and move this block of code to a function in
 > EventManager (seems to me the most logical place, unless you have
 > another suggestion). One comment though -- This code only exists to
 > handle both named Query and Custom Query. From what I understand,
 > named query will soon be removed, and once you remove the named
 > query part of this code, it is essentially a just single function
 > call. Should I still move it to another class?

Yeah, if it's soon to be replaced with a one-liner, then no point in moving it.  In that case, just need to fix the formatting and remove tabs and trailing spaces.

Patch Set 2:

 > >
 > > (4 comments)
 > >
 > > I don't think we want to delete these other test files
 > Yes, I that happened by mistake. I will rebase to patch 1. Also,
 > how do I use the formatter for .drl files?

Not sure, I have the drools editor installed, but it doesn't appear to provide a re-format option.  However, besides fixing it manually, you can always do it a kludgy way: copy it to a java file, use the java formatter, and then copy it back.

My changes are under the latter one, which is somehow not reflected here. Its not showing that file as changed when I go git-status. Can I submit a new review?
